title: Real Estate Deals in Syracuse NY: The Investor's Complete Guide for 2024
excerpt: Syracuse, NY is quietly becoming one of the Northeast's most attractive markets for real estate investors, with low entry prices and strong rental demand. This guide covers the best neighborhoods, deal types, and strategies for finding profitable real estate deals in Syracuse NY.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Syracuse NY
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Syracuse NY. Explore top neighborhoods, average prices, wholesale, flip & rental strategies for 2024 investors.

sections.content: Syracuse, New York doesn't make the national headlines the way Phoenix or Nashville do. But quietly, this mid-sized Rust Belt city has been catching the attention of savvy real estate investors who know how to spot value before the crowd arrives. With home prices well below the national median, a large student population, and serious revitalization underway, real estate deals in Syracuse NY offer some of the best returns in the entire Northeast corridor. Whether you're just getting started or you're an experienced investor expanding your portfolio, this guide will show you exactly what's happening in the market and how to profit from it. If you're brand new to the game, bookmark our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide first — it'll give you the foundation you need before diving into a specific market.
Syracuse NY Market Overview: Why Investors Are Paying Attention

Syracuse sits in the heart of central New York and is home to roughly 145,000 people. It's an anchor city for Onondaga County, which has a broader metro population of around 650,000. The city is home to Syracuse University, Upstate Medical University, and a growing healthcare and tech sector. These institutions create a steady, year-round demand for housing — particularly rentals — that many smaller cities simply can't match. The local economy has also benefited from recent federal investments in semiconductor manufacturing, with Micron Technology announcing a massive chip plant in nearby Clay, NY. That project alone is projected to bring tens of thousands of jobs to the region over the next decade, which could dramatically reshape housing demand across the entire metro area.
Average Home Prices and What Your Money Gets You

This is where Syracuse really shines for investors. As of 2024, the median home price in Syracuse sits around $155,000 to $175,000 — compared to the national median of roughly $420,000. That means you can acquire single-family rentals, duplexes, and small multifamily properties at prices that make the numbers work with far less capital than you'd need in coastal markets. Entry-level investment properties can still be found in the $60,000 to $100,000 range in certain neighborhoods, while renovated turnkey rentals in better areas fetch $180,000 to $250,000. For flippers, distressed properties are regularly available in the $40,000 to $80,000 acquisition range with ARVs (after repair values) between $130,000 and $200,000 — leaving healthy margins for experienced operators.

Before making any offer in Syracuse, run the numbers using the free [ARV Calculator at aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator). Plug in comparable sales from nearby sold listings to make sure your estimated after-repair value is grounded in real market data — not wishful thinking.
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Syracuse

Not all zip codes in Syracuse are created equal. The city has distinct pockets of opportunity depending on your investment strategy. Here's a breakdown of the neighborhoods investors are watching most closely right now.
Eastside / University Hill

This area surrounding Syracuse University is ground zero for student rental demand. Vacancy rates here are extremely low, and properties within walking distance of campus command premium rents. Two- to four-unit multifamily properties here are the most sought-after asset class. Expect to pay more — acquisition prices run $150,000 to $300,000 — but gross rent multipliers often justify the higher price point.
Northside / Near Northside

The Northside is one of Syracuse's most interesting emerging neighborhoods. It has a tight-knit community, improving infrastructure, and real price appreciation momentum over the past three years. Single-family homes here are still relatively affordable at $80,000 to $140,000, making it a sweet spot for buy-and-hold rental investors and flippers who can buy distressed properties and resell to owner-occupants.
Westside / Strathmore

Strathmore offers a more stable, working-class neighborhood with consistent rental demand from families and healthcare workers employed at nearby Upstate Medical. Properties here tend to be in better condition, which reduces renovation risk for newer investors. It's an ideal area for anyone looking for steady cash flow without heavy rehab projects.
Southside

The Southside carries higher risk but also the highest upside for wholesale and flip investors. Acquisition prices can dip below $50,000 for distressed properties, and with the right renovation budget, exit prices in the $120,000 to $150,000 range are achievable. This is a market for experienced investors with strong contractor relationships and solid cost control.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Syracuse
Wholesale Deals

Wholesaling is alive and well in Syracuse. The city has a high rate of distressed and vacant properties, which means motivated sellers aren't hard to find with the right lead generation strategy. Assignment fees of $5,000 to $15,000 per deal are common, with some high-equity deals producing $20,000 or more. If you're newer to this strategy, start with our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide to understand the legal and logistical fundamentals before approaching sellers.
Fix and Flip

Fix-and-flip investors can find strong margins in Syracuse, particularly on the Northside and parts of the Eastside. A typical flip might involve buying a distressed three-bedroom home for $65,000, putting $40,000 into renovations, and selling for $155,000 — producing a gross profit of roughly $50,000 before carrying costs and closing fees. The key is accurate ARV estimation and tight contractor management. Use our free [Wholesale Deal Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to model your deals before committing.
Buy-and-Hold Rentals

Long-term rental investing is arguably the strongest play in Syracuse right now. Average rents for single-family homes range from $900 to $1,400 per month, while two-unit duplexes can generate $1,400 to $2,200 combined. When you acquire properties at the price points available in this market, cash-on-cash returns of 8% to 14% are genuinely achievable — numbers that are nearly impossible to find in more expensive markets.

The best real estate markets aren't the ones on the front page of the Wall Street Journal. They're the cities where the fundamentals are strong, the prices are fair, and most investors haven't looked yet. Syracuse is exactly that kind of market right now.
Syracuse Real Estate Market Trends to Watch in 2024

items: Micron Effect: The $100 billion Micron chip plant investment near Syracuse is creating long-term job growth expectations that are starting to influence housing demand and investor activity across the metro.
Rising Rents: Average rents in Syracuse have climbed roughly 12% to 18% over the past two years, increasing cash flow potential for landlords who bought earlier.
Low Inventory: Like most U.S. markets, Syracuse is experiencing constrained housing inventory, which is pushing retail home prices upward and creating more competition for available properties.
Investor Interest Rising: More out-of-state investors are discovering Syracuse, which means off-market deal sourcing is becoming increasingly important to stay ahead of the competition.
University-Driven Stability: Syracuse University enrollment remains strong at 22,000+ students, providing a permanent floor under rental demand in key neighborhoods near campus.

sections.content: How to Find Off-Market Deals in Syracuse

The best real estate deals in Syracuse NY are rarely listed on the MLS. They come from motivated sellers who haven't listed their homes yet — distressed owners, estates, absentee landlords, and pre-foreclosure situations. To find these deals consistently, you need a repeatable off-market lead generation system. Direct mail, driving for dollars, cold calling, and building relationships with probate attorneys are all proven approaches. For a complete breakdown of these strategies, read our guide on [How to Find Off-Market Real Estate Deals](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/off-market-real-estate-deals) — it covers every major channel with step-by-step instructions.
Tips for Out-of-State Investors Targeting the Syracuse Market
A growing percentage of Syracuse real estate investors are based outside of New York. If you're investing remotely, here's what you need to know to do it successfully.

items: Build a local team first: Before you buy anything, lock in a reliable property manager, a general contractor with local market experience, and a real estate attorney familiar with New York landlord-tenant law.

Understand New York's landlord laws: New York is a tenant-friendly state. Eviction processes can take months in Onondaga County, so screen tenants rigorously and work with a property manager who knows local procedures.

Visit before you buy: Plan at least one in-person trip to walk neighborhoods and meet your team face-to-face. Photos and Google Street View don't tell the full story, especially in neighborhoods with higher distress levels.

Use data to analyze every deal: Don't rely on gut feel when you're investing from a distance. Run every potential deal through a structured analysis framework — our guide on [How to Analyze Real Estate Deals Like a Pro](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/how-to-analyze-real-estate-deals) walks you through exactly how to do that.

Check city-specific rental regulations: Syracuse has property registration requirements and periodic rental inspections through the city's code enforcement office. Factor inspection fees and any required upgrades into your acquisition analysis.
Focus on neighborhoods with institutional anchors: Properties near SU, Upstate Medical, or SUNY ESF tend to have more predictable demand and are easier to manage remotely due to stable tenant pools.

sections.content: If you're evaluating the Syracuse market from out of state, browse current deal activity and property data at [aiwholesail.com/markets/syracuse-ny](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/syracuse-ny) to get a real-time picture of what's available and what deals are moving in the market right now.
Is Syracuse Right for Your Investment Strategy?

Syracuse is not a glamorous market. It doesn't have the brand recognition of New York City or the appreciation headlines of Austin. What it does have is affordability, consistent rental demand, a structural economic catalyst in the Micron development, and entry price points that make cash-flowing deals genuinely achievable without needing millions in capital. For wholesalers, flippers, and buy-and-hold rental investors who prioritize fundamentals over hype, the real estate deals in Syracuse NY represent exactly the kind of opportunity that builds lasting wealth. The investors who move early — before this market gets saturated — will be the ones who look back and say they got in at the right time.
